workflow.name: Build an omnichannel OTP verification flow
workflow.description:
**How it works**


• Receives user identity data from multiple channels (WhatsApp, Telegram, email).
• Generates and stores secure one-time passwords (OTP).
• Validates OTP codes against Postgres records.
• Centralizes user identity across channels.
• Maintains session continuity for onboarding and verification.

**Set up steps**

• Import the workflow into n8n.
• Configure Postgres or Supabase credentials.
• Download and create the required database tables in Supabase.
• Connect messaging channel credentials.
• Define OTP expiration and validation rules.
• Test using sample user verification scenarios.

workflow.name: 1. User verification OTP Onmichannel Workflow N8N

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO onboarding_sessions (channel, platform_user_id, step)
VALUES ($1, $2, 'waiting_for_email')
ON CONFLICT (channel, platform_user_id)
DO NOTHING;

UPDATE onboarding_sessions
SET step = 'waiting_for_otp'
WHERE channel = $1
AND platform_user_id = $2;

parameters.html:
=

Your verification code

Your code is:

{{ $('Generate OTP').item.json.otp_code }}

This code expires in 5 minutes.

Este código expira en 5 minutos.
parameters.subject: Tu código de verificación

parameters.query:
SELECT *
FROM user_otps
WHERE channel = $1
AND platform_user_id = $2
AND otp_code = $3
AND is_used = false
AND expires_at > now()
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 1;

UPDATE onboarding_sessions
SET step = 'verified'
WHERE channel = $1
AND platform_user_id = $2;

SELECT global_user_id, email
FROM users
WHERE global_user_id = $1
LIMIT 1;

UPDATE onboarding_sessions
SET email = $1,
    step = 'waiting_for_otp'
WHERE channel = $2
AND platform_user_id = $3;

INSERT INTO user_channels (global_user_id, channel, platform_user_id)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
ON CONFLICT (channel, platform_user_id)
DO UPDATE SET global_user_id = EXCLUDED.global_user_id
RETURNING global_user_id, channel, platform_user_id;

nodes.name: Check if the channel already exists

parameters.query:
SELECT global_user_id
FROM user_channels
WHERE channel = $1
AND platform_user_id = $2;

assignments.name: response_message
assignments.value: ={{ `✅ Channel already verified for ${$json.email}. Continuamos con tu perfil.` }}

parameters.query:
SELECT step, email
FROM onboarding_sessions
WHERE channel = $1
AND platform_user_id = $2;

assignments.name: response_message
assignments.value: 👋 Welcome to Zyntha ☕ To continue, I need your email address to create or verify your profile.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO user_otps (channel, platform_user_id, otp_code, expires_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4);

assignments.name: response_message
assignments.value: I sent you a verification code. Enter it here to continue.

assignments.name: response_message
assignments.value: ❌ Invalid code. Please try again with the correct code.

parameters.query:
SELECT email
FROM onboarding_sessions
WHERE channel = $1
AND platform_user_id = $2
LIMIT 1;

assignments.name: response_message
assignments.value: ⚠️ I couldn’t find the session email. Please send your email to continue.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO users (email, verified)
VALUES ($1, true)
ON CONFLICT (email)
DO UPDATE SET verified = true, updated_at = now()
RETURNING global_user_id, email;

assignments.name: response_message
assignments.value: ✅ Verification completed. This channel has been linked to your user.

parameters.content:
## Omnichannel OTP Verification & User Onboarding

### How it works
This workflow centralizes user verification across WhatsApp, Telegram, and email using OTP validation.

It detects the channel, manages onboarding sessions, generates secure OTP codes, and links verified channels to a single global user identity.

### How to set up
1. Import the workflow into n8n.
2. Configure Postgres or Supabase credentials.
3. Download and create the required database tables (users, user_channels, user_otps, onboarding_sessions).
4. Connect email and messaging channel credentials.
5. Test with new and returning users.

### Customization
• Adjust OTP expiration time.
• Modify onboarding steps or validation logic.
• Add additional communication channels.

## Section 1 — Channel Detection & Normalization

Receives incoming requests from multiple platforms and identifies the communication channel.

Normalizes user data into a unified structure before checking if the channel is already linked.

## Section 2 — Existing Channel Validation

- Checks if the incoming channel is already associated with a verified user.

- If found, skips the verification process and continues with the existing profile.

## Section 3 — Session Initialization

- Creates or retrieves the onboarding session for the current channel.

- Tracks the current step of the verification process.

## Section 4 — Email Extraction & Validation

- Extracts a valid email from the user message using regex detection.

- If no email is found, prompts the user to provide one before continuing.

## Section 5 — OTP Generation & Delivery

- Generates a secure one-time password and stores it with an expiration timestamp.

- Sends the OTP via email and updates the session to waiting_for_otp.

- A confirmation message is sent to the email used for delivering the OTP.

## Section 4 — Email Extraction & Validation

- Extracts a valid email from the user message using regex detection.

- If no email is found, prompts the user to provide one before continuing.

- A message is sent to the user if the code does not exist, asking them to verify it and send it again.

## Section 7 — Identity Consolidation

- Marks the OTP as used and updates the session as verified in the Supabase database.

- Links the verified channel to a single global user ID to ensure omnichannel continuity.

- A confirmation message is sent via email if the verification has been completed and successfully linked to the user.

## DATABASE REQUIRED

This workflow depends on predefined Postgres tables.

You must create users, user_channels, user_otps, and onboarding_sessions before running the flow.

## Security Notice
- Do not log OTP codes in production environments.

- Always define an expiration time and mark codes as used after validation.

main.node: Check if the channel already exists

user.name: Yenire

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
